observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended to the management to:
Instruct the Municipal Assessor, the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the differences between their respective records on a periodic basis to arrive at the correct real property tax and special education tax collectible balances, in order to enhance the reliability and accuracy of the financial statement accounts.
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entry to fairly present the actual tax collectibles on the Statement of Financial Position based on the certified list of tax delinquencies prepared by the Municipal Treasurer.
Direct the MAssO to expedite the completion of the RPU inventory to validate the existence of real properties and eliminate duplicate or outdated tax declarations. This will ensure that only valid and collectible real property tax receivables are recorded in the assessment rolls.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the management to direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV), supported by verifiable documents, to record the two motor vehicles that have not yet been recorded in the books of accounts.
Further, we reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendations to the management to:
Instruct the Inventory Committee, the GSO-Designate, and the Municipal Accountant to continue its conduct of physical count each year and to exert extra efforts in reconciling the reports per physical inventory, per GSO, and per accounting records.
Expedite the disposal of unserviceable heavy equipment and motor vehicles to prevent further deterioration and ensure proper PPE management. Likewise, the management to avail the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts in accordance with COA Circular 2020-006 to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.
Establish good practices on supply and property management by extending efforts to update and maintain complete records for supply and property accountabilities, such as property tags and property cards per category of PPE.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the management to:
Require the Municipal Accountant to faithfully adhere to the accrual basis of accounting for insurance and fidelity bond premiums and to draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) supported with verifiable working papers to effect the necessary adjusting entries, in order to enhance the fair presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements.
Resolve its manpower concerns by formally designating a staff member responsible for tracking the expired and unexpired portions of prepaid transactions and ensuring compliance with the accrual basis of accounting.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the management to:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately issue demand letters to concerned Punong Barangays and Barangay Treasurers on the immediate submission of liquidation reports in relation to the unliquidated financial assistances given by LGU Baungon. Proper filing and documentation of these demand letters to barangays should be established.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to constantly remind the concerned barangay officials of their responsibility to submit liquidation reports as recipients of funds transferred from LGU Baungon. Emphasize that barangays with unliquidated balances are ineligible for additional funding until prior grants are liquidated.
Henceforth, as a help to barangay and considering their capacity in handling liquidation of funds received, require the MEO to provide the MAccO with copies of the 100% accomplishment reports and inspection reports for all LGU-funded projects, in order for the MAccO to take initiative in following up those barangays with unliquidated balances.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the management to strengthen its internal control over inventory management by:
Establishing a dedicated storage facility to ensure the safekeeping and accountability of inventory items before issuance to end-users. Once in place, all purchases must be recorded as inventory upon acquisition in accordance with standard accounting procedures.
Implementing a proper issuance and recording system by ensuring that inventory items are released only upon request through a duly approved Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS). The General Services Officer (GSO) must consolidate these transactions by preparing Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) on a weekly basis to provide an accurate record of issued supplies.
Ensuring proper documentation of inventory expenses by requiring the Municipal Accountant (MAccO) to prepare Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) based on the SSMI and RIS, which must be submitted weekly by the GSO to the MAccO.
Directing the GSO and MAccO to maintain Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Card, respectively, to properly account for purchases, issuances, and balances of all inventory items.
Requiring the Inventory Committee to conduct semi-annual physical inventory counts and prepare a Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI). This report must be submitted to the MAccO and GSO to reconcile their respective records. Submit the same to the audit team by July 31 of the current year and January 31 of the following year for the first and second semesters, respectively.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the management to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant and her subordinates to immediately submit all outstanding Disbursement Vouchers and General Journal accounts (including all Journal Entry Vouchers), in compliance with applicable rules and regulations to avoid issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance and modification of auditor’s opinion on financial statements due to audit scope limitation. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant to ensure that complete supporting documents are completely forwarded to COA for post-audit and verification.
Implement adequate measures that will enable the agency to meet such deadlines, such as but not limited to expediting the segregation and compilation of financial documents.
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer’s Office (MTO) fast track the retrieval and immediate submission of all remaining Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) in their custody to the Municipal Accounting Office. Additionally, the MTO should implement measures to ensure the timely turnover of all required documents in the future to prevent unnecessary delays.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the management to:
Submit immediately to the audit team the noted lacking documents to substantiate the disbursed transactions; otherwise, recomputation of the correct BAC honorarium will be based on the actual documents attached to the claim. Any excess or overclaim thereat will be subjected to refund or issuance of ND;
Stop the improper practice of granting honoraria to the BAC members without adhering to the prescribed maximum honorarium rates per successfully completed procurement; and
Consider establishing written internal rules and procedures for granting honoraria based on attendance or participation in procurement activities and meetings, so that that only individuals who have successfully completed their tasks or participated in activities will be compensated.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the management to:
Secure and submit to the audit team a written authority from the DILG Regional Director explicitly approving the benchmarking-related foreign travels of selected municipal officials to South Korea and Japan, including the other lacking relevant documentary requirements. Otherwise, refund the total disbursed amount totaling ₱1.447 million;
Submit written valid justification/s for the necessity of conducting benchmarking activities in South Korea and Japan, supported with prior proposals/feasibility studies made, AIP, and CapDev Agenda directly addressing or responding to the CapDev needs of the LGU. Further, justify that the four (4) conditions required by DILG-DBM JMC No. 2 dated September 23, 2016 were met.
Remind the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and all approving signatories of Disbursement Voucher to ensure that benchmarking activities are specifically defined or disclosed in the LGU’s CapDev Agenda; and,
Refrain from conducting “lakbay aral” activities abroad which can be addressed by a more feasible and more economical mode of CapDev intervention that can be undertaken locally.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the management to:
Submit immediately the relevant lacking documents on foreign travel expenses incurred to facilitate our further audit action. Secure documents from LMP-Bukidnon Chapter regarding the breakdown of paid registration fees to accurately account the same, including the pertinent documents necessary for the proper liquidation thereof. Non-submission by management may warrant issuance of audit suspension or disallowance, as the case may be;
Ensure that expenses related to foreign travel be recorded to Travelling Expenses – Foreign account; and,
Strictly observe prudence and judiciousness in the utilization of public funds.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the management to take the following actions:
Require the Sangguniang Bayan, through its Secretary, to submit a valid justification on the reasonableness of the amount expended for meals and snacks during its regular sessions, by taking into consideration the quantity availed per session.
Discontinue the immoderate quantity of the number of meals and snacks during regular sessions to ensure the prudent and frugal use of government funds.
Remind the Sanggunian and the BAC to stop its incorrect practice of requiring deliveries for meals and snacks before the conduct of procurement; and,
Submit immediately the noted lacking documents to the audit team to fully substantiate the disbursed transactions and for the continuance of the post-audit of the said transactions.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the management to:
Submit immediately to the audit team the noted lacking documents to substantiate the disbursed transactions. Henceforth, ensure that complete supporting documents are attached as prescribed under the Memorandum of Agreement, DOH AO No. 2022-001, and DOH-DBM JAO No. 2023-001.
Submit immediately individual certifications from those concerned recipients with unsigned payrolls certifying that the same were duly received and stating the amount, purpose and period of claim; otherwise, let the disbursing officer refund the amount of P144,000.00.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the management to limit the cash advance of confidential fund for three (3) months requirement only, in strict adherence to the provisions of the foregoing joint circular dated January 8, 2015. Likewise, promptly secure the required certification of POP from RPOC for the timely utilization of appropriation for confidential expenses.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the management through the BAC to prepare and submit the required APPs, semestral PMR and APCPI to the GPPB in the prescribed formats within the prescribed period to ensure full compliance with the reportorial requirements set forth under Section 12.2 of the IRR of RA 9184 and applicable GPPB circulars.

We recommended to the management to remind the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare, henceforth, reliable SCBAA and SAAOB reports by including as part of obligations the already transferred unspent appropriations for LDRRMF- MOOE which have been treated already as a trust liability in the TF’s books of accounts at year-end. Further, we required them to prepare a corrected SCBAA and SAAOB for CY 2023 for the management’s consumption/reference, supported with the necessary Obligation Requests for the said transfer, so as not to improperly treat such balances as surplus available for supplemental budget.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation to the management that the Municipal Accountant should immediately draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV), supported with verifiable documents and working papers, in order to record/adjust those valid reconciling items reflected in the BRS and submit to the audit team for review and evaluation. Moreover, ensured that reconciling items are recorded/adjusted in the books of accounts immediately upon detection.
observations.reason: Compliance documents are yet to be submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to require the Municipal Accountant to perform the following: 

1. Draw the necessary adjusting entries, supported with relevant working papers, in the TF’s and GF’s books of accounts for the following transactions:

a. In the TF’s books, the transferring of “CIP” account and “Advances to Contractors” account from TF to GF to close the “Due to NGAs” account, applicable for the two projects;
b. In the GF’s books, the receipt of transferred “CIP” account and “Advances to Contractors” account from TF to GF, applicable for the two projects;
c. In the GF’s books, the correcting entry to adjust the understated CIP account and to close the already recouped Advances to Contractor during the final billing; and,
d. In the GF’s books, the reclassification of CIP to its proper asset account for the already completed project located at Barangay Liboran.

2. Include a separate relevant disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements under PPE accounts for the transferred “CIP” account from TF to GF for the project: Water System Reservoir at Barangay Lingating funded under LGSF- FALGU.

Moreover, the management to immediately take necessary measures, especially by creating an ad hoc committee who will assess thoroughly the status and the feasibility of the uncompleted Water System Reservoir project located at Barangay Lingating. After due consideration and deliberation, the management to allocate appropriations for its completion so that it can be utilized for its intended purpose.

observations.reason:
Unsettled relocation surveys and inaction of former Municipal Assessor. No separate note disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements as required by IPSAS 17.

However, the management complied the following recommendations: 

1. The GSO recently has a safe vault to take custody of TCTs and other relevant documents on parcels of land. 

2. JEV # 101-24-03-1035C was already drawn to derecognize portion of land donated to PNP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to expeditiously coordinate with the Provincial Government of Bukidnon for the execution of the Deeds of Donation for the two aforecited projects so that the rightful ownership thereof can be transferred in favor to the municipality. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw the necessary adjusting journal entries, supported with verifiable documents, to derecognize the subject assets recorded in the GF’s books of accounts while simultaneously record back such assets to Trust Fund’s book pending execution of such deeds.
observations.reason:
The following JEVs of necessary adjusting entries were submitted to the audit team, to wit:

1) JEV #101-24-03-1035B;
2) JEV #101-24-01-414C.

However, per verification,  we have noted that the Deed of Donation was not submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following: 

1. The Municipal Accountant’s Office to make the necessary adjustments to derecognize those properties recorded under the General Fund’s books of accounts of the municipality instead to the recipient barangays, including those affected accounts. 

2. Record back the said assets to the Trust Fund’s books of accounts pending execution of Deed of Donation.

3. The management to coordinate with the Provincial Government, which is the source agency, to execute deed of donation for turn over to the barangay recipients.
observations.reason:
The Municipal Accountant has already drawn the necessary adjusting journal entries under the General Fund in derecognizing the subject projects. 

However, per verification,  we have noted that the Deed of Donation was not submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to take the following actions:

1) Require each concerned MTO personnel to submit valid justification and/or the original certificates of appearance evidencing the veracity of the travels incurred and paid for those dates in question.

2) Stop the improper reproduction of Certificates of Appearance to be used as supporting attachments for travel claims associated with bank transactions with LBP-Carmen. Instead, original CAs should be requested from the said agency to substantiate such claims.

3) Prior authorization of all travels by the Local Chief Executive, meticulously assess first whether or not such official travel is essential to the effective performance of an official or employee’s mandates/functions. Likewise, consider attendance to league officers’ meetings as “official time” only rather than on “official business” and should not be granted with travel claims charged against municipal funds.

4) Effect the refund of ₱22,900.00 from the concerned personnel with excessive travel payments. Henceforth, exercise prudence on the utilization of scarce government funds by preventing the incurrence of unnecessary and excessive travel-related expenditures.

We recommended to the management to submit immediately the aforementioned lacking documents for our further review and audit action. Moreover, the management was reminded to diligently adhere with the necessary documentation requirements for Hazard Pay and Subsistence Allowance claims. Additionally, we suggested that payment of these benefits should be contingent upon the determination of actual work rendered by personnel and through reimbursement basis only.

We recommended to the management to establish clear guidelines on communication expenses by defining a ceiling/limit for the amount, number of accounts, and eligible personnel entitled to such reimbursement. Additionally, assess whether the charges incurred in the statement of accounts are allowable and align with organizational needs.

We recommended to the management to stop the improper practice of granting honoraria to the members of the BAC Secretariat engaged in regular full-time functions in the BAC Office and to the invited NGO observers. Instead, the management may consider granting overtime pay or per diem to the former and latter, respectively, if warranted.

Likewise, we recommended to the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately to the audit team the noted lacking documents to substantiate the disbursed transactions. Henceforth, ensured that complete supporting documents are attached as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2023-004.

We recommended to the management to strictly require the BAC Secretariat to furnish all the perfected contracts, along with their related documents, in earliest time possible and to meticulously review the completeness and orderliness of the awarded contracts based on the provided contract review and technical review checklists prior submission to the audit team. Further, submit immediately the enumerated documents and/or valid justifications for some noted observations for our auditorial and legal review. 

More so, submit immediately the complete documentary requirements for the technical review of those infrastructure-related contracts using the checklist provided by the audit team, in which such documents are relevant to facilitate the technical evaluation thereof to be conducted by the COA Regional Technical Audit Group (RTAG).
observations.reason:
Inaction of management, specifically by the former BAC Secretariat.

Delays were still observed for contracts received by the audit team in CY 2024. Moreover, the lacking documentary requirements are still not submitted as to date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to require the Municipal Accountant to immediately draw Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV), supported with verifiable documents and working papers, in order to properly reclassify transactions lumped to liability accounts and submit immediately to the audit team for verification and post-audit. observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to:

Submit a legal basis for the grant of the ₱4,643,750.00, allegedly as COVID-19 assistance, to all its Officials and regular employees, job orders, and other personnel not employees of the LGU-Baungon; 

Upon submission or presentation of the legal basis of the grant, please submit the following documents to the Office of the Auditor for verification of the validity and propriety of the claim:

Submit the appointments/contracts of services of regular employees, Job Orders, and other personnel, and the certified list of eligible JOs/Cos and regular employees from the HRMO.
Justify the selective grant of COVID-19 Assistance that includes even those not considered as low-income households based on actual basic monthly pay. 
Explain the use of RA 11469 or the Bayanihan to Heal as One Act and RA 11494 or the Bayanihan to Recover as One Act which had already expired in June 2020 and June 2021 respectively. 

We further recommended to the management that thereafter all special purpose/time-bound activities are debited under the “Advances to Special Disbursing Officer” Account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to re-send positive confirmation letters to debtors in their last known registered addresses. Otherwise, management should consider the filing of requests for write off with complete documents to the Commission on Audit.

The management has experienced difficulties in fully implementing the recommendation. Some debtors could not be located in their last known addresses. Office of the Municipal Agriculturist has tried to re-send positive confirmation letters but only few responded and located. For those located and identified individuals, the management plans for recontracting of debt as mentioned during the exit conference.

Likewise, the management has yet to gather documentary requirements for the request for write-off to COA for some items of receivables, especially those pertaining to deceased individuals.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to be prudent and judicious in exercising its fiscal responsibility and accountability over the disbursed funds, by strictly adhering to the proper obligation and disbursement processes. Specifically, the management should ensure that all DVs, payrolls and obligation requests (ObRs) are completely signed by proper authorities in order to delineate full accountability over the disbursed funds. If in case that a representative is authorized to sign on behalf of the proper official, he/she should print his name and full signature to the pertinent document and becomes fully accountable therefor. 

For those disbursements with unsigned DVs, payrolls and ObRs, we recommended to the management to submit a certification that the concerned authorized official failed to sign the document and is fully responsible and accountable thereof. The details of the disbursed transaction and the nature of his participation thereof should also be specified. Non-submission thereof may warrant issuance of audit suspensions or disallowance, as the case may be.

For those documents signed by an unknown representative, we recommended to the management to submit a certified list of signature specimens of those authorized/allowed to sign in behalf of the concerned officials. Submit also a joint certification from the proper official and his/her representative that they are fully responsible and accountable to such disbursement. The details of the disbursed transaction and the nature of their participation should also be specified. Non-submission thereof may warrant issuance of audit suspensions or disallowance, as the case may be.

Further, we recommended to the management to implement a good internal control system to prevent incurrence of such improper disbursement practice with inadequate signatures.

We recommended to the management to ensure that disbursement vouchers for infrastructure-related disbursements in favor to contractors should be sufficiently and adequately attached with the required documents as prescribed under Section 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001. Specifically, proper coordination among offices involved in the processing of these claims should be made, especially the Municipal Engineering Office, BAC and the Municipal Accounting Office. The latter office should meticulously review and ensure that these pertinent documents are completely attached to DVs prior disbursement. 

Moreover, we recommended to the management to submit immediately to the audit team the lacking supporting documents and valid justification/s on other noted observations for our further review and evaluation.

We recommended to the management to require the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, BAC Chairman, BAC Secretariat and those other concerned personnel, to submit the lacking supporting documents to the audit team for post-audit, in order to conclude the appropriateness, legality and validity of the disbursed transactions. For those disbursements with other noted deficiencies, relevant documents and/or valid justifications from the concerned personnel/offices should also be submitted for our evaluation and consideration.

Moreover, we required the Municipal Accountant, henceforth, to meticulously and sufficiently support disbursement vouchers with required pertinent documents prior to payment and submission to audit team. Further, compute and effect the collection of liquidated damages for those delayed procurements vis-à-vis the agreed delivery period per contract.
observations.reason: The municipality prospectively applied the audit recommendation on ensuring disbursement vouchers to be adequately supported with required pertinent documents prior to payment and submission to audit team. However, the lacking supporting documents stated in the audit report were not yet submitted to the audit team for post-audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to stop the granting of honoraria not supported with enough legal basis and to ensure, henceforth, that complete documents are attached to each disbursement voucher. Further, the management to set the authorized monthly honorarium per official thru the issuance of a legislative resolution in compliance with Section 447(a)(I)(XI) of RA 7160.

We recommended to the management to meticulously and judiciously plan the procurement process of the agency by consolidating the quarterly requirements for similar procurement items based on the PPMP to be incorporated in the APP.

We recommended to the management to be prudent and judicious in exercising its fiscal responsibility and accountability over the disbursed funds, by strictly adhering to the proper obligation and disbursement processes. Specifically, the management should ensure that all DVs, payrolls and obligation requests (ObRs) are completely signed by proper authorities in order to delineate full accountability over the disbursed funds. If in case that a representative is authorized to sign on behalf of the proper official, he/she should print his name and full signature to the pertinent document and becomes fully accountable therefor.

For those disbursements with unsigned DVs, payrolls and ObRs, we recommended to the management to submit a certification that the concerned authorized official failed to sign the document and is fully responsible and accountable thereof. The details of the disbursed transaction and the nature of his participation thereof should also be specified. Non-submission thereof may warrant issuance of audit suspensions or disallowance, as the case may be.

For those documents signed by an unknown representative, we recommended to the management to submit a certified list of signature specimens of those authorized/allowed to sign in behalf of the concerned officials. Submit also a joint certification from the proper official and his/her representative that they are fully responsible and accountable to such disbursement. The details of the disbursed transaction and the nature of their participation should also be specified. Non-submission thereof may warrant issuance of audit suspensions or disallowance, as the case may be.

Further, we recommended to the management to implement a good internal control system to prevent incurrence of such improper disbursement practice with inadequate signatures.

observations.recommendation:
In view hereof, we recommended to the municipal management to consider the following remedial measures:
	
1.Establish proper time schedule in the preparation and submission of PPMPs by respective municipal offices (end-users) to the Municipal Budget Office for funding purposes.

2.Endeavor to come up with an APP that is timely, accurate and useful to the operations and to come up with a consolidated RFQ for similar items based on time schedule as indicated in the PRs of respective department or offices.

3.Endeavor to identify the mode of procurement and undergo consolidated or bulk procurement on similar items based on consolidated RFQ, so as to minimize processing on procurement, budgeting, approval, delivery and payment.  As well as encourage participation of legitimate and known suppliers in the locality or nearby city.  

Further, we recommend that municipal management should institute effective monitoring measures to eradicate delays on procurement processes where significant time had been consumed, most particularly the on the delivery time.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurers Office already submits monthly duplicate copies of issued ORs together with corresponding RCDs to the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to stop the practice of issuing check to the first name in the payroll and payment of travelling expenses thru payroll.

We recommended that management advice the Municipal Accountant to require the complete documentary requirements needed for all government transactions to prevent suspension in audit of the paid transactions.

We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Treasurer/GSO to indicate the complete terms and conditions in the purchase orders issued to protect the interest of the municipality and to attach a copy of the ARE in the purchases of equipment.
We recommended that management instruct the Human Resource Management Officer to observe the guidelines in approving and certifying leave credits available for monetization.

We recommended that management adhere strictly to the provision of maintaining a net take home pay, to boost the employees’ morale and productivity.  If possible the Municipal Accountant or the approving committee should take into consideration the capacity to pay of the borrower.
Require the Municipal Accountant to consider the accrual of interest expenses on long term loans of the agency before closing its books of accounts at year end.
observations.managementAction: Long term loans was fully paid last November 2016.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements any encumbrances on or restriction in the use of agency’s asset such as property, plant and equipment pledged as collateral for the agency’s long- term loans.

Require the Disaster Action Officer to include in the Physical and Financial Accomplishment Report the utilization of the net accumulated LDRRMF and other dedicated disaster reduction and management resources in the previous years, and the Municipal Accountant to discuss or disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements of the agency the amount and details of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: Disclosed as a separate attachment incorporated in the submitted FS and Notes.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the BAC Chairman to comply the requirements set forth under Section 12.12 of the IRR of RA 9184.
observations.reason: Not all departments and offices submits  PPMP to BAC for consolidation in the APP.
